name: Image Database Manager (PyQt5)
description: PyQt5 GUI that ingests JSON image records, builds dynamic tables, resolves folder paths across naming conventions, and opens matching directories.
longDescription: Users maintain a JSON-defined image catalog; the app renders sortable, filterable tables, builds candidate folder paths using several naming strategies (space, underscore, concatenated, model-only), checks that paths exist, and opens the system file manager when a match is found. Settings persist in `settings.json`; optional packaged builds bundle icons and backgrounds.
features: Error surfacing when paths are missing
Optional build script with asset bundling
highlights: Solves messy on-disk naming conventions with explicit strategies
Clear validation messages when paths or data are invalid
challenges: Filesystem assumptions break when assets move between drives or OS locales.
solutions: Configurable settings, resilient path checks, and clear errors before shelling out to Explorer/Finder.
security: Never publish decryption passwords in public documentation or marketing material
Validate JSON sources before executing filesystem operations
Sanitize paths to avoid traversal surprises
